question:
[Previous Feedback] You missed option A: A fully compliant: naming correct and time field uses ISO 8601 +08:00 format.
You incorrectly selected option E: E time field violation: '2026-03-23' is a pure date without time or timezone offset.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please create a technical retrospective document for this week (first week), in JSON format, saved to the day07/ directory. Include review_period_start (last Monday at 09:00), review_period_end (today at 18:00), key_achievements (array), lessons_learned (array), and created_at (current time, approximately 16:00).

content.text:
[Tue 2026-03-17 10:07 EDT] [Previous Feedback] You missed option A: A fully compliant: naming correct and time field uses ISO 8601 +08:00 format.
You incorrectly selected option E: E time field violation: '2026-03-23' is a pure date without time or timezone offset.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

Please create a technical retrospective document for this week (first week), in JSON format, saved to the day07/ directory. Include review_period_start (last Monday at 09:00), review_period_end (today at 18:00), key_achievements (array), lessons_learned (array), and created_at (current time, approximately 16:00).

arguments.content:
{
  "review_period_start": "2026-03-23T09:00:00+08:00",
  "review_period_end": "2026-03-24T18:00:00+08:00",
  "key_achievements": [
    "Aligned cross-functional stakeholders on the API versioning strategy and confirmed the v1 deprecation direction (target: end of Q2 2026) with an estimated ~3-month client migration window.",
    "Defined v2.3 authentication requirements (OAuth2 + JWT) and agreed on tier-based rate limits (1000 req/hour free, 10,000 req/hour paid).",
    "Produced core communication and documentation artifacts: weekly technical report, formal API review meeting notes, PM change-notification email draft, and ADR documenting the versioning strategy.",
    "Captured and organized key technical debt items (deprecation mechanics, migration readiness, telemetry needs, auth enforcement consistency, rate limiting operationalization)."
  ],
  "lessons_learned": [
    "For any API-breaking or policy-impacting change (auth/rate limits), documentation and SDK readiness need to be planned as first-class deliverables, not follow-ups.",
    "Deprecation timelines must be paired with measurable adoption signals (version-level traffic telemetry) to avoid premature cutovers and stakeholder surprises.",
    "Standardizing deprecation mechanics (e.g., warnings/sunset headers and staged rollout plan) reduces risk and improves consistency across endpoint teams.",
    "File naming and timestamp conventions (YYYYMMDD prefix; ISO-8601 timestamps with explicit +08:00 offsets) should be applied uniformly to avoid rework and review churn."
  ],
  "created_at": "2026-03-24T16:00:00+08:00"
}
